objective: Deliver a company-private thin Web mode backed by the official Codex agent runtime, then compare it fairly with the existing Haystack/DuckDB backend on the same synthetic HVAC corpus, model endpoint and complex questions.

input: one natural-language project question or a named high-frequency workflow
an isolated project copy containing approved documents and read-only datasets
process: Codex plans iterative evidence gathering inside an isolated workspace
immutable named tools expose bounded read-only database operations
a required local MCP server exposes only three fixed read-only database operations outside the command sandbox
the same existing Chat UI renders the final Markdown and original-filename citations
output: bounded tool activity without private chain-of-thought
measured comparison evidence against the existing backend

selection.sdk_status: The official TypeScript SDK was verified, but its current Windows child-process finalizer and the nested Codex Desktop job boundary made result capture unreliable in this environment. The SDK wraps the same CLI, so the prototype uses the official CLI transport directly.
selection.provider: custom OpenAI-compatible Responses provider with supports_websockets=false
selection.data_tool: official MCP Python SDK 1.28.1 plus DuckDB 1.5.4 with three fixed read-only operations and no model-supplied SQL or path
selection.policy: Windows elevated setup is installed but failed the host-source negative read; WSL2 Landlock with Codex 0.144.5 is the validated replacement candidate and is not yet integrated
selection.ui: reuse the current single-Chat page; no fifth visual architecture

measured_evidence.claim: The company Responses endpoint works with Codex when configured as a custom provider with supports_websockets=false.
Codex completed the complex data-quality plus load-weighted-COP question using two immutable governed tools and targeted project documents.
measured_evidence.result: turn.completed; HP-02 missing/frozen, HP-03 duplicates and highest COP, HP-04 out-of-order

measured_evidence.claim: The existing backend failed the equivalent MX01 case after its tools succeeded because the final numeric grounding gate rejected the answer.
measured_evidence.result: hard_gate_pass=false; refused=true; no table or citations

measured_evidence.claim: The official MCP Python SDK exposes only three fixed read-only HVAC tools and returns the frozen COP result.
measured_evidence.evidence: local official MCP STDIO smoke on 2026-07-18

measured_evidence.claim: The remediated adapter creates a fresh session per request, keeps DuckDB outside command read permissions, validates structured citations, and removes raw thread IDs.
Focused Web and existing-backend regressions remain green after the secure runtime refactor.
Secure real-model acceptance cannot run on this PC until the elevated Windows sandbox setup receives administrator approval.
measured_evidence.result: unelevated rejected restricted reads; elevated helper requested an administrator setup prompt

measured_evidence.claim: The Web service now fails closed until an operator probe proves that the generated permission profile reads the copied workspace and denies both the private DuckDB file and application source outside the workspace.
measured_evidence.result: schema-v2 marker is written only after allow=0, private-deny=73 and application-source-deny=73; every schema-v1 marker is rejected

measured_evidence.claim: Final independent code rereview found no remaining Critical or Important implementation findings.
measured_evidence.evidence: bounded secure_codex_rereview on the 2026-07-18 working tree
measured_evidence.result: Critical=0; Important=0; not merge-ready only because secure real-model/UAC acceptance is still blocked

measured_evidence.claim: The authorized elevated Windows setup completed, but the sandbox account could still open application/root-repository files outside the copied workspace.
measured_evidence.evidence: local OS probes on 2026-07-18 after codex sandbox setup --elevated --current-user
measured_evidence.result: setup exit=0; private DuckDB with explicit ACL exit=73; root repository read exit=0; Windows production startup remains blocked

measured_evidence.claim: The existing WSL2 environment enforces the intended allow-only read boundary with the official Codex 0.144.5 Linux binary.
measured_evidence.evidence: local WSL2 Landlock probe using a custom Permission Profile
measured_evidence.result: workspace allowed exit=0; outside private file exit=1; mounted Windows root repository exit=1

safety_boundaries: No company data, credentials, endpoint values, runtime indexes or model sessions enter Git.
The browser never receives an API key.
Each request receives a fresh ephemeral project copy; hidden benchmark truth and repository internals are excluded.
DuckDB remains outside the command sandbox and is reachable only through a required official MCP server with three fixed read-only operations.
A custom Permission Profile grants only minimal runtime reads, one copied document workspace, temporary writes, and no command network access.
Native Windows deployment requires the elevated sandbox and fails closed when that administrator-approved setup is unavailable.
Web search and direct network access are disabled inside project-analysis turns.
OnionQuant Cron, context_state, Inbox, Outbox and runtime state remain untouched.
Existing untracked evaluation results remain untouched.

slices.title: Current research and real runtime feasibility probe
TDD implementation of isolated workspace, provider, JSONL runtime and governed tools
Reuse existing Chat UI with runtime switch and named workflow buttons
Frozen complex benchmark, browser acceptance and human review
slices.blocker: Windows elevated sandbox failed the outside-source negative read. WSL2/Landlock passed the prototype boundary but still needs production integration.

slices.title: Deployment handoff, independent review, verification, commit and push

current_blocker: The authorized Windows elevated sandbox is installed but cannot isolate readable host drives on this PC. Secure real-model acceptance now requires explicit approval and implementation of the validated WSL2/Landlock backend.
